rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MANUEL AND LUCY ABEYTA FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Confirmation Of Successor Trustee; To Remove Trust From Continued Court Supervision; And To Unblock Blocked Account
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/29/23. (No earlier availability.) All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] Unless an exception applies, such beneficiaries must either receive or waive notice. A supplement to the petition must be filed to address this issue. Notice must include the language for remote appearance procedures, as required by LRSF 14.1.D.6. A courtesy copy of the supplement and the proof of service (and any other papers filed since the petition and consent pertaining to this petition) must be received by the assigned examiner BEFORE 3/20/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Compliance Status Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. No corrected inventory and appraisal has been filed to address the following issues: 1) The conservatee's interest in the real property (1258 Geneva Ave, SF) must be listed on Attachment 2 and appraised by the probate referee. 2) The IRA must be listed on Attachment 2 and appraised by the referee, unless the IRA solely consists of cash. 3) The contents of the safe deposit box and tangible personal items listed on Attachment 2 must be appraised by the referee. New GC-040 & 041 forms must be filed. Based on the reported value ($240,353), a bond or blocked account may be required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARK ALAN WINKLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/13/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: There was no waiver of notice attached as part of Exhibit B, which was just the consents to the notices of proposed action. The proof of service filed on 9/19/22 was incomplete at item 2 and the Notice of Hearing did not include the language for remote appearance procedures, as required by LRSF 14.1.D.6. Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc., not just currently. There were 1,500 shares of Mind Medicine Mindmed reported on the I&A. There are only 100 shares now on hand. Notwithstanding a waiver of account, the report must list the information required by law, including information as to sales, purchases, or exchanges of assets and changes in the form of assets, [CRC 7.550(b).] Merely reporting that "holdings changed slightly" is not sufficient. The administrator does not disclose whether cash was invested in interest-bearing accounts as required by Probate Code Sect. 9652(a). It also appears that he maintained well over the FDIC insured amount in one bank account. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/2/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCINE MARIE DOUGLASS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar for faiulre to address the following outstanding issues (e-mailed to counsel on 11/02/2022): 1) Item 2 of the acknowledgment of receipt section of the DE-147 form must list the petitioner's telephone number, in addition to his address. The date of signing must be on or after 11/02/2022. An amended DE-147 form must be filed. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form. New notice of a continued hearing must be mailed to Monica Douglass. 3) A supplement verified by the petitioner (not by the attorney) to provide the following must be filed: i) Whether item 3f(1) of the petition should have been checked. ii) Whether Monica Douglass is the only child the decedent ever had. iii) Whether the petitioner consents to a bond of $1,305,000, as the amount must be rounded up to the nearest thousand dollars. See LRSF 14.16(L). 4) The first response to calendar notes filed on 10/6/2022 must be verified by the petitioner (not by the attorney). 5) Unless a nomination can be obtained from Monica Douglass, the petitioner has no priority for appointment as he is not an heir under Probate Code section 8462, in which case notice must be given to the San Francisco Public Administrator having higher priority than the petitioner. If the Public Administrator declines to act, does the petitioner have standing to bring this petition in the absence of any interest in the estate (other than statutory compensation as personal representative? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN S. JACKSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. The judicial officer waives the clerical error in the hearing date in the published notice. Status of closing the estate is set for 2/14/2024. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 2/14/24. Appearance will be required on 2/14/24 if no petition has been filed. No SASE was provided; copies of signed orders cannot be returned to Counsel.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ADRIAN LEONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/15/2023. Additional notes were e-mailed to counsel on 01/23/2023.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE CHARLIE WRIGHT REVOCABLE TRUST, U.D.T DATED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Bring Trust Under Court Supervision, Revoke Powers Of Trustee, And Appoint Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANDREW HAMILTON BURKE TONKEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 10/21/17. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status of closing the estate will be set for 2/14/24. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 2/14/24, no appearance will be required on that date. (The proposed Letters that were submitted do not have a wet signature.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD C. BLUM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/27/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: a. Issues posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 1/9/23 have not yet been addressed. Is the petitioner (either FRHI Real Estate Holding (US) Inc. or Dianne Feinstein?) going to withdraw this petition now that a will is being proffered? b. No proposed order. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/17/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD C. BLUM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/27/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: a. No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 and 14.59 E.] b. It does not appear that Michael Klein fully executed the amended petition. c. No proof of publication. d. Both mailed and published notice must clearly indicate that an AMENDED petition has been filed. [CRC 7.53 a.] e. A proposed non-resident personal representative must file a declaration setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate for the Court to determine the amount of the bond. [LRSF 14.16 F.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/17/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
